p: Keep your production line running at peak performance with Isobex’s tailored maintenance plans. Designed specifically for industrial fibre laser systems, our service contracts provide the precision, reliability, and technical assurance manufacturers depend on. Whether it’s scheduled servicing, emergency support, or performance optimisation, we help you eliminate downtime and extend the lifespan of your equipment.

Located at our UK facility, Isobex provides comprehensive maintenance solutions for fibre laser systems across all major industrial models. Our engineers use calibrated diagnostic tools and follow a strict technical framework to ensure every system performs at factory standards — safely, efficiently, and consistently.
We offer fully tailored maintenance plans to suit your production environment and operational load, with options for Essential, Priority, and Premium coverage tiers.
Optical alignment verification to maintain beam accuracy
Cooling system checks to ensure temperature stability
Laser power calibration and performance optimisation
Safety and interlock inspection for compliance assurance
Whether you run a single workstation or manage an entire production facility, Isobex ensures your systems remain fully operational with minimal interruption to your workflow.

Every service is carried out by qualified engineers with extensive experience in fibre laser systems. We combine proactive maintenance with rapid on-site response to prevent costly disruptions and performance loss.

From installation support to long-term maintenance, we take a hands-on approach throughout the full service cycle. This allows us to deliver an unmatched combination of technical precision, professional service, and operational reliability — ensuring your equipment continues to meet production demands without compromise.

Our experts will reply you within 24 hours

h1: FLEXIBLE FINANCING FOR YOUR BUSINESS INVESTMENT
p: We are pleased to be able to introduce you to Time Finance Plc, an independent business finance provider with offices across the UK. They specialising in funding assets such as our lasers, and they can help make your investment more manageable with flexible financing options.
Key Benefits of Financing Your Equipment:
Protect Your Cash Flow – Spread the cost of new equipment, avoiding large upfront expenses.

Preserve Credit & Banking Facilities – Keep existing credit lines and cash reserves available for other business needs. Unlike overdraft facilities, finance agreements cannot be withdrawn unexpectedly.
Let the Equipment Pay for Itself – Finance payments align with the benefits your chamber brings, ensuring a self-sustaining investment.

Fixed Payments for Stability – Enjoy predictable, fixed payments throughout the agreement, making budgeting and forecasting easier. Unlike bank loans tied to interest rates, your payments won’t fluctuate.
Fast & Simple Approval – A quick phone call is all it takes—most decisions are made within 24 hours.
100% Tax-Deductible – Finance lease rentals can be fully deducted against your business profits.

/privacy-notice.title: Industrial Fiber Laser Cutting Machines | Isobex UK
/privacy-notice.description: Discover high-performance fiber laser cutting machines for sheet, tube, and plate processing. Precision engineering and expert UK support from Isobex.
p: At Isobex Lasers Ltd, we respect your privacy and are committed to protecting the personal data we hold about our business contacts, suppliers, and customers. This Privacy Notice explains how we collect, use, and store personal data in accordance with the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR) and the Data Protection Act 2018.
Isobex Lasers Ltd (“we”, “our”, “us”) is a UK-registered company providing industrial laser equipment, parts, and maintenance services.
Registered Address: 8 Swift, Tamworth, Staffordshire, B77 2RP
We may collect and process the following types of business-related personal data:

• Name and job title• Business email address and telephone number• Company name and postal address• Communication preferences and correspondence history• Details required for quotes, contracts, and service delivery
We process personal data for the following purposes:

• To manage business relationships and provide products or services• To issue quotations, invoices, and contractual documents• To communicate with customers and respond to enquiries• To carry out maintenance, support, and account administration• To send relevant business-to-business marketing communications about our products, services, and updates
Under UK GDPR, we process personal data based on the following lawful grounds:

• Legitimate Interests – for managing business relationships and direct B2B marketing communications• Contractual Necessity – to perform or prepare a contract for our goods or services• Legal Obligation – to retain records for accounting, tax, and regulatory compliance
We may contact you by business email or telephone with information about our products and services that may be relevant to your company.
From time to time, we may use trusted third-party service providers to assist with marketing communications or customer database management.
These providers act strictly under our instructions and are bound by data processing agreements to ensure full UK GDPR compliance.
You can opt out of receiving marketing communications at any time by emailing privacy@isobexlasers.co.uk or using the unsubscribe option (where available).
We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes for which it was collected:

• Contract and customer records – up to 6 years after the end of the relationship• Marketing contacts and leads – up to 2 years from last contact, unless you request otherwiseAfter these periods, data is securely deleted or anonymised.
We do not sell or rent personal data to third parties.
We may share limited information with trusted service providers (such as IT, marketing, or accounting support) under strict data processing agreements.
Appropriate technical and organisational measures are in place to safeguard all data against unauthorised access, alteration, or loss.
Under UK GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

• To request access to the data we hold about you• To request correction of inaccurate or incomplete information• To request erasure of your personal data, where applicable• To object to processing for legitimate interests or direct marketing• To lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) at www.ico.org.uk
We may update this Privacy Notice periodically to reflect changes in legal or operational requirements. The latest version will always be available on request or on our website.
If you have any questions about this Privacy Notice or how your data is handled, please contact us at:privacy@isobexlasers.co.uk
Isobex Lasers Ltd, 8 Swift, Tamworth, Staffordshire, B77 2RP

h2: DECADES OF SERVICE IN THE INDUSTRY.
h3: OUR MISSION IS TO KEEP YOU CUTTING.
p: At Isobex Laser Supplies Ltd, we know that in manufacturing, every minute of downtime costs money. That’s why we’ve built our business to keep your production moving. Since our founding, we’ve combined cutting-edge laser technology with hands-on engineering expertise to deliver industrial laser systems you can trust.

Our team of skilled engineers doesn’t just deliver machines — we provide installation, training, tailored maintenance, and rapid-response support. With Isobex, you’re not just buying equipment; you’re gaining a partner committed to maximising uptime, reducing costs, and driving efficiency.
Since the beginning, Isobex has recognised the gaps in industrial service. Long lead times, poor support, and costly breakdowns were common across the sector — and we set out to change that.
Our team of engineers combines deep technical expertise with hands-on service, ensuring every machine we install continues to run at maximum efficiency.
From the very beginning, Isobex set out to fix what the industry was missing: fast response, reliable service, and engineers who truly understand the machines.

Instead of leaving manufacturers waiting weeks for parts or struggling with poor aftercare, we built a service-first model. Our engineers provide installation, training, tailored maintenance, and rapid support — ensuring your machines stay cutting at their best.
For us, it’s not just about supplying advanced industrial lasers. It’s about giving businesses the confidence that downtime won’t hold them back.

We believe laser technology should empower manufacturers, not hold them back. That’s why we deliver state-of-the-art laser systems for sheet & tube cutting, tailored maintenance packages that fit your production schedule and rapid response support to minimise downtime.
With Isobex, you don’t just get a supplier — you get a partner dedicated to maximising uptime and efficiency.

p: Why will my  machine will not start

Possible cause: Power, safety interlock, or protection fault.What to check:- Main power switch is on.- Emergency stop is released.- All safety doors are fully closed.- Check for error messages on the screen.- Ensure protective lenses and covers are fitted correctly.
The laser is not cutting through the material
The machine is not responding to the software
The laser fires but does not follow the design correctly
The material is burning or charring
The laser does not fire at all
The cut position is offset or misaligned

h2: Pay for Itself in Less Than a Year
Take Control of Your Running Costs
How Much Are You Spending on Nitrogen?
Replace Ongoing Costs With One Smart Investment
Why Buy Your Compressor Through Isobex
Want to See Your Potential Savings?
p: If you're using nitrogen bottles for your laser cutting machine, you already know how quickly the costs add up.
A typical nitrogen bottle may only provide around 40 minutes of cutting — meaning you're constantly replacing bottles, interrupting production, and watching your profits disappear.
What if you could eliminate that ongoing cost completely?
With Isobex Laser-Configured Air Compressors, you can.
Compressed air is not suitable for every laser cutting application, but where viable it can significantly reduce ongoing operating costs.
Using nitrogen for laser cutting might seem like the standard solution — but it's expensive, inefficient, and inconvenient.
Ongoing monthly expense that never stops
You're not just buying nitrogen — you're committing to a never-ending cost.
Isobex Lasers offers specially formulated and configured compressors designed specifically for laser cutting applications.
Designed for clean, dry air output
Built for reliability and continuous use
Engineered to integrate with laser machines
This means better performance, lower costs, and less downtime.
Many laser users are spending hundreds — sometimes thousands — every month on nitrogen.
When you switch to an Isobex compressor:
For many customers, the system pays for itself in under 12 months — sometimes much sooner.
One of the biggest frustrations with nitrogen bottles is running out halfway through a job.
Just reliable performance when you need it.
Nitrogen prices go up.Delivery charges increase.Supply delays happen.
This is about future-proofing your business.
If your nitrogen bottle lasts around 40 minutes, the costs can add up faster than you think.
Now multiply that by your cost per bottle…
Even at modest prices, you're likely spending hundreds — sometimes thousands — every month on nitrogen alone.
With an Isobex Laser-Configured Air Compressor, you can:
✔ Eliminate ongoing nitrogen costs✔ Reduce downtime✔ Increase productivity✔ Take control of your running costs

Yes — compressed air is a popular and cost-effective alternative to nitrogen for many laser cutting applications. Isobex compressors deliver clean, dry air suitable for many cutting requirements.
